texts.full_text: whereas CD300a and CD300f behave as inhibitory receptors by recruiting SHp-1 phosphatasThe human body's intricate免疫系统 relies on balancing activating and inhibitory signals to respond appropriately to threats while avoiding harm from overactivity or misdirection. Central to this balance are the CD300 family of proteins, a group of type I transmembrane molecules primArily found on myeloid lineage cells originating in bone marrow—such as macrophages. This family comprises seven members located near each other on chromosome 17q25.1, with some acting as activators and others as inhibitors. Activating receptors like CD300b,
